comprehension_gate.instruction:
READ THIS ENTIRE PROMPT before posting your comprehension gate. Then READ every file listed in required_attachments. Then POST a comprehension gate comment on Vikunja Task 82, prefixed:
[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]
Your comprehension comment MUST contain ALL of the following sections (verbatim section headers required, in this exact order, no numbered prefixes):
A. MILESTONE OBJECTIVE (in your own words, 3-5 sentences) B. WORK ITEMS (one sentence each for ALL 11 WIs — do NOT summarize, do NOT group) C. FILES TO CREATE (list every new test file exactly as specified in deliverables) D. FILES TO MODIFY (list every existing test file you will add tests to) E. FILES TO READ (all production source files you will read for context) F. DELIVERABLE STRUCTURE (recite VERBATIM: branch name; each test class name and test count; ledger path + frontmatter) G. ORACLE EXPECTATION (recite the exact git diff command and expected EMPTY output) H. QUALITY GATE FLOOR (state the 982-test floor and the new-test minimum of 20) I. RISKS AND AMBIGUITIES (address each risk I.1–I.8 from this prompt) J. PRODUCTION CODE PROHIBITION ACKNOWLEDGMENT (quote the L-15 prohibition verbatim)
After posting, STOP. Do NOT proceed to implementation until you receive [agent:sdo][phase:comprehension-review] VERDICT: APPROVED.

milestone.objective: Extend the Sprint 8 test suite to cover coverage gaps in shared/ and launcher/ components. EA-4 targets: shared/src/runtime_config.py (4 untested functions), launcher/guest_deploy.py (_validate_vsock_topology 8 failure branches + _validate_guest_runtime_configs 3 failure scenarios), launcher/__main__.py (_run_uat2_prompt_flow_preflight + _cleanup teardown), launcher/vm_manager.py (request_elevation), shared/schemas/car.py (new test_car.py), and 6 untested IPC message type convenience encoders. No production code is modified.
milestone.ledger_frontmatter: --- ledger_id: <YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint8_ea4_shared-launcher-hardening date: <YYYY-MM-DD> sprint_id: 8 entry_type: EA predecessor: 20260422_210246_sprint8_ea3_ui-hardening branch: feature/p5-task8-ea4-shared-launcher-hardening merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
milestone.depends_on: EA-3 merged to main. Verified: merge commit 12fd0ba (feature/p5-task8-ea3-ui-hardening), auto-merged by Co-Lead at ad311ac. 962 tests passing (baseline). If main has advanced further, use the current main HEAD.

item.title: TestResolveServiceRoot — 2 tests: normal Python interpreter and PyInstaller frozen path
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (MODIFY EXISTING or CREATE if absent) Class: TestResolveServiceRoot
Test 1 (normal Python): Call resolve_service_root() in a standard Python environment where sys.frozen is not set (or is False). Assert the returned path is a real directory that exists on disk (Path.is_dir() == True). Assert the returned path is an ancestor of or equal to the repo root (i.e., contains a known marker file or directory such as pyproject.toml).
Test 2 (PyInstaller frozen): Monkeypatch sys to set sys.frozen = True and sys._MEIPASS = "/fake/meipass". Assert resolve_service_root() returns Path("/fake/meipass") or the expected frozen-path computation. Verify no real filesystem access is needed by using a fully monkeypatched sys.
RISK I.1 — PyInstaller monkeypatching: sys.frozen and sys._MEIPASS must be set atomically in a single monkeypatch context. Use monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "frozen", True) and monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "_MEIPASS", "/fake/meipass", raising=False). Restore properly via the monkeypatch fixture teardown.

item.title: TestResolveDeploymentMode — 3 tests: explicit parameter, environment variable, default
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (same file as WI-1) Class: TestResolveDeploymentMode
Test 1 (explicit parameter): Call resolve_deployment_mode(mode="host"). Assert the return value equals DeploymentMode.HOST (or the equivalent enum/string).
Test 2 (environment variable): Monkeypatch os.environ["BLARAI_DEPLOYMENT_MODE"] = "guest". Call resolve_deployment_mode() with no argument. Assert the return value equals DeploymentMode.GUEST.
Test 3 (default): Ensure environment variable is absent (monkeypatch.delenv if present). Call resolve_deployment_mode() with no argument. Assert the return value is the expected default (read source to determine whether default is HOST, GUEST, or raises). If default raises, test the exception.
RISK I.2 — env isolation: monkeypatch.setenv / monkeypatch.delenv are required to isolate tests from the real environment. Do NOT use os.environ directly in test body without monkeypatching — the test may fail if BLARAI_DEPLOYMENT_MODE is set in the CI environment.

item.title: TestParseDeploymentMode — 3 tests: "host", "guest", invalid string
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (same file as WI-1/2) Class: TestParseDeploymentMode
Test 1 ("host"): Call parse_deployment_mode("host"). Assert return equals DeploymentMode.HOST.
Test 2 ("guest"): Call parse_deployment_mode("guest"). Assert return equals DeploymentMode.GUEST.
Test 3 (invalid): Call parse_deployment_mode("invalid_mode"). Assert the function raises ValueError (or the specific exception type documented in source). Use pytest.raises(ValueError) context manager.
Read source to confirm the exact function signature, enum members, and exception type before writing tests.

item.title: TestBuildFailureFingerprint — 2 tests: structure verification and required keys
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_runtime_config.py (same file as WI-1/2/3) Class: TestBuildFailureFingerprint
Test 1 (structure): Call build_failure_fingerprint(stage="TEST_STAGE", code="TEST_CODE", message="test message"). Assert the return value is a dict. Assert dict contains keys: "stage", "code", "message", "disposition", "fail_closed". Assert "disposition" == "FAIL" and "fail_closed" == "true" (or True — read source for exact type).
Test 2 (required keys): Call build_failure_fingerprint with a different code and stage combination. Assert the returned dict keys are exactly the expected set (no extra keys, no missing keys). Use set equality on dict.keys().
Read source to confirm the exact return structure before writing tests.

item.title: TestValidateVsockTopologyFailures — 8 tests covering all vsock failure branches
item.detail:
File: launcher/tests/test_guest_deploy.py (MODIFY EXISTING) Class: TestValidateVsockTopologyFailures
Read _validate_vsock_topology() in launcher/guest_deploy.py fully. Enumerate ALL failure branches (returns with an error code / raises). The SDO analysis identified 9 distinct error codes across guest_deploy.py. Identify which ones are emitted by _validate_vsock_topology() specifically.
For each failure branch, write one test that: 1. Mocks the vsock/socket layer so no real network call is made 2. Triggers the specific failure condition (e.g., connection refused, timeout, wrong CID, wrong port, protocol mismatch) 3. Asserts the function returns the expected error code or raises the expected exception 4. Asserts no side effects (no real socket opened)
RISK I.3 — vsock evidence path: _validate_vsock_topology() may write to an evidence file path. If so, monkeypatch the path to a tmp_path fixture location to avoid polluting the real filesystem. Use the tmp_path fixture from pytest.
RISK I.4 — service imports: guest_deploy.py may import from launcher services that require additional mocking at module import time. Use conftest.py-level mocks or module-level monkeypatching if needed. Do not import guest_deploy without verifying all its dependencies are mockable.
Write all 8 tests as individual test methods in the class. No parameterized bundling — each branch gets its own method for failure isolation.

item.title: TestValidateGuestRuntimeConfigsFailures — 3 tests: PA fail, AO fail, both fail
item.detail:
File: launcher/tests/test_guest_deploy.py (same file as WI-5) Class: TestValidateGuestRuntimeConfigsFailures
Read _validate_guest_runtime_configs() in launcher/guest_deploy.py. This function validates configuration for both the Policy Agent (PA) and Assistant Orchestrator (AO) services.
Test 1 (PA fail): Provide a config where the PA configuration is invalid (wrong type, missing required field, or invalid value — read source for validation rules). Assert the function returns the PA-specific error code or raises with PA context.
Test 2 (AO fail): Provide a config where the AO configuration is invalid. Assert the function returns the AO-specific error code or raises with AO context.
Test 3 (both fail): Provide a config where both PA and AO configs are invalid. Assert the function reports both failures (or the first failure, depending on implementation — read source for fail-fast vs accumulate behavior).
Mock any external calls (file I/O, subprocess, vsock) to keep tests unit-level.

item.title: TestRunUat2PromptFlowPreflight — 2 tests: success path and exception path
item.detail:
File: launcher/tests/test_main.py (MODIFY EXISTING or CREATE if absent) Class: TestRunUat2PromptFlowPreflight
Read _run_uat2_prompt_flow_preflight() in launcher/__main__.py fully. This is the deployment smoke test that validates gateway handshake, session creation, token streaming, and pgov evaluation.
Test 1 (success): Mock all external dependencies (UI gateway connection, session store, pgov evaluation) to return success values. Call _run_uat2_prompt_flow_preflight(). Assert the function returns True (or the success sentinel — read source). Assert all expected mock calls were made.
Test 2 (exception): Mock one of the external dependencies to raise an exception (e.g., ConnectionRefusedError or RuntimeError). Call the preflight function. Assert it returns False or re-raises (read source for exception handling). Assert the failure is logged or recorded as an error-fingerprint.
RISK I.5 — __main__ import: importing launcher/__main__.py may trigger side effects at import time (argparse setup, signal handlers, etc.). Use importlib.import_module or mock the entry-point guard. Alternatively, if the function is already tested via a conftest fixture that handles the import, reuse that pattern. Read existing test_main.py before deciding approach.

item.title: TestCleanupAtExit — 5 tests covering service guards, session store, and vm_was_started
item.detail:
File: launcher/tests/test_main.py (same file as WI-7) Class: TestCleanupAtExit
Read _cleanup() in launcher/__main__.py fully. This is the atexit handler that stops running services and the VM.
Test 1 (services running, vm_was_started=True): Mock service objects as running, mock VM manager. Call _cleanup(). Assert both services stopped and VM stopped.
Test 2 (services not running, vm_was_started=True): Mock service objects as not running (already stopped). Call _cleanup(). Assert service stop NOT called, VM stop IS called.
Test 3 (services running, vm_was_started=False): Mock services as running. Call _cleanup() with vm_was_started=False (or equivalent). Assert services stopped, VM stop NOT called.
Test 4 (session store cleanup): Assert session store teardown is called if a session store is active. Read source for whether _cleanup() calls session store close/teardown.
Test 5 (no services present / None guards): Call _cleanup() with no services initialized (None objects). Assert no AttributeError raised — guard handles None.
RISK I.6 — cleanup globals: _cleanup() may read module-level globals. If so, monkeypatch the module globals directly. Use monkeypatch.setattr(main_module, "_pa_service", mock_service) pattern. Identify the module globals from source before writing tests.

item.title: TestRequestElevation — 2 tests: success path and failure path
item.detail:
File: launcher/tests/test_vm_manager.py (MODIFY EXISTING or CREATE if absent) Class: TestRequestElevation
Read request_elevation() in launcher/vm_manager.py fully. This function requests Windows UAC elevation (or equivalent) when the process is not running as admin.
Test 1 (success): Mock the subprocess or ctypes call that triggers elevation. Assert the function calls the elevation mechanism and returns the expected success value (True, 0, or the subprocess result — read source).
Test 2 (failure): Mock the subprocess or ctypes call to raise an exception or return a failure code. Assert request_elevation() returns False or raises the expected exception with the correct error fingerprint.
RISK I.7 — ctypes Windows-only: request_elevation() likely uses ctypes.windll which only exists on Windows. Tests must NOT import ctypes directly. Mock at the ctypes.windll.shell32 level: monkeypatch.setattr("ctypes.windll.shell32.ShellExecuteW", mock_func). Alternatively, if vm_manager wraps the ctypes call behind a helper, mock the helper. Read source to determine the correct mock surface.

item.title: CREATE shared/tests/test_car.py — canonical_hash determinism, is_complete, DecisionArtifact, enums
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_car.py (CREATE NEW) Class(es): TestCanonicalHash, TestIsComplete, TestDecisionArtifact, TestEnums
Read shared/schemas/car.py fully before writing any tests.
TestCanonicalHash: Test 1 (determinism): Create two identical CAR objects. Assert canonical_hash() returns the same value for both. Test 2 (sensitivity to field changes): Create a CAR, hash it. Change one field (e.g., resource). Hash again. Assert the two hashes differ.
TestIsComplete: Test 1 (complete CAR): Create a CAR with all required fields populated (source_agent, destination_service, resource, request_id). Assert is_complete() returns True. Test 2 (incomplete — missing required field): Create a CAR with one required field missing or None. Assert is_complete() returns False. Test 3 (empty CAR): Create a minimal/default CAR. Assert is_complete() returns False.
TestDecisionArtifact: Test 1 (construction): Create a DecisionArtifact with known fields. Assert all fields are accessible. Assert car_hash matches the hash of the source CAR.
TestEnums: Test 1 (ActionVerb members): Assert all expected ActionVerb values are present (enumerate from source — READ source, do NOT guess). Test 2 (Sensitivity members): Assert all expected Sensitivity values are present. Test 3 (AdjudicationDecision members): Assert ALLOW, DENY, ESCALATE are present (or whatever the source defines).

item.source: shared/src/ipc_types.py or equivalent — message type encoders
item.title: IPC message type convenience encoders — 6 tests for HANDSHAKE_REQUEST/RESPONSE, PROMPT_REQUEST, STREAM_TOKEN, PGOV_RESULT, GENERATION_COMPLETE
item.detail:
File: shared/tests/test_ipc_message_types.py (CREATE NEW) or add to existing test file covering IPC types — read existing test coverage first.
Read shared/src/ipc_types.py (or equivalent) to identify the 6 untested convenience encoder functions/methods for these message types: - HANDSHAKE_REQUEST - HANDSHAKE_RESPONSE - PROMPT_REQUEST - STREAM_TOKEN - PGOV_RESULT - GENERATION_COMPLETE
For each encoder, write one test that: 1. Calls the encoder with representative arguments 2. Asserts the returned message has the correct message_type field 3. Asserts the payload fields match the input arguments 4. Asserts the encoded message is serializable (round-trip via JSON or the wire protocol's encode/decode methods if present)
RISK I.8 — EA-4 sizing: 6 tests (one per encoder) plus 3 enum tests from WI-10 is the minimum for WI-11. Write thorough tests rather than trivial assertions. If an encoder has multiple call signatures, test each variant.

constraint.detail: Zero production source files may be modified. Specifically: NO edits to shared/src/**, services/**/src/**, launcher/*.py (non-test files), or pyproject.toml sections other than test configuration. If a production code change is required to unblock a test, STOP and report in the completion report. ORACLE check: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea4-shared-launcher-hardening --name-only | grep -vE "tests|conftest|docs|pyproject" → EMPTY.
Do NOT rename, move, or restructure existing test files. Add new test classes or methods to existing files in-place. New test files (test_car.py, test_ipc_message_types.py) are additive.

Do NOT append to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (frozen at Entry 52). Write a NEW per-file entry at docs/ledger/<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>_sprint8_ea4_shared-launcher-hardening.md. Predecessor: 20260422_210246_sprint8_ea3_ui-hardening.
Do NOT add new packages to pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] or [project.dependencies]. Tests must use only existing dependencies (pytest, pytest-asyncio, pytest-mock, unittest.mock).

constraint.rule: No real vsock or socket connections
constraint.detail: All transport and vsock tests must mock the connection layer. No test may establish a real socket or vsock connection. This is enforced by the test execution environment (CI host does not have vsock support).
Do NOT add new `__init__` test hooks, dependency injection points, or constructor parameters to production code to make tests easier. Test through existing public interfaces and monkeypatching.
Sprint 9 writes docs/governance/**. Sprint 8 writes **/tests/**. Working sets are disjoint. Do NOT touch any docs/governance/ file in this EA.

Write tests ONLY for the 11 WI targets listed above. Do not extend scope to other untested functions discovered during source reading. If additional gaps are found, document them in the completion report for SDO to prioritize in EA-5.

risk.resolution: sys.frozen is not a real attribute in standard Python — monkeypatch must use raising=False to avoid AttributeError on setattr. Use: monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "frozen", True, raising=False) monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "_MEIPASS", "/fake/meipass", raising=False) Monkeypatch teardown is automatic via pytest fixture. Do NOT permanently modify sys at module level.

Use monkeypatch.setenv("BLARAI_DEPLOYMENT_MODE", value) and monkeypatch.delenv("BLARAI_DEPLOYMENT_MODE", raising=False) for all environment-variable-dependent tests. Never read or write os.environ directly in test bodies. pytest's monkeypatch fixture guarantees rollback after each test.

If _validate_vsock_topology() writes evidence to a path, identify the path constant from source. Use tmp_path fixture: monkeypatch the path constant to tmp_path / "evidence.json". Do NOT allow writes to the real evidence location (C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\... or the VM path).

launcher/guest_deploy.py may import from services/ or shared/ in a way that requires runtime-available resources. Read the import block at the top of guest_deploy.py. If any import triggers side effects, add module-level mocks in the test file's conftest.py block or use pytest's monkeypatch on sys.modules before import. Do NOT let a bare `import launcher.guest_deploy` cause a test collection failure.

launcher/__main__.py contains the CLI entry point. Importing it may trigger argparse.ArgumentParser.parse_args() or signal.signal() registrations. Before importing, patch sys.argv = ["__main__"] and mock signal.signal if needed. Read the existing test_main.py for how prior tests handle this — replicate the same import isolation pattern.

_cleanup() reads module-level mutable globals (_pa_service, _ao_service, _vm_manager, etc. — verify exact names from source). For each test in TestCleanupAtExit, explicitly set the relevant module globals via: import launcher.__main__ as main_module monkeypatch.setattr(main_module, "_pa_service", mock_service) Reset to None in teardown (monkeypatch handles this automatically).

ctypes.windll does not exist on non-Windows platforms. Tests must mock at the correct level. Read vm_manager.py to identify the exact ctypes call surface. Likely pattern: monkeypatch.setattr("ctypes.windll.shell32.ShellExecuteW", mock_func) If vm_manager wraps ctypes behind a module-level variable or helper, mock the wrapper instead. Never import ctypes.windll in the test file body.

The quality gate floor is 982 passed (962 + 20 minimum new tests). The WI breakdown yields 2+3+3+2+8+3+2+5+2+6+6 = ~42 tests if all branches are covered. The 20-test floor is a minimum, not a ceiling. Write complete tests for each branch — do not truncate to hit exactly 20. The "mature not minimal" directive applies.

quality_gate.gate: All new/modified test modules must import without error: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI python -c "import shared.tests.test_runtime_config; print('OK')" python -c "import shared.tests.test_car; print('OK')" python -c "import shared.tests.test_ipc_message_types; print('OK')" python -c "import launcher.tests.test_guest_deploy; print('OK')" python -c "import launcher.tests.test_main; print('OK')" python -c "import launcher.tests.test_vm_manager; print('OK')" Any ImportError is a gate failure.
Run the targeted test slice: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/tests/ launcher/tests/ --tb=short -q All new tests must pass. No pre-existing tests may regress.

Run the full suite to verify no cross-service regression: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q Floor: 982 passed, 2 skipped. Current baseline is 962; minimum new tests = 20. Any failure in services/ or ui_gateway/ or ui_shell/ test directories is a regression.

Verify no production files were modified: git diff main...feature/p5-task8-ea4-shared-launcher-hardening --name-only | grep -vE "tests|conftest|docs|pyproject" Expected output: EMPTY. Any services/src/, shared/src/, launcher/*.py (non-test) path in the filtered diff is an L-15 violation. STOP and report.

verification.description: Verbatim terminal commands the Lead Architect can run to confirm success. Run from C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI in PowerShell.

commit_template.format: tests(task82/ea4): Sprint 8 shared + launcher test hardening (WI-1..WI-11, ~42 tests)
commit_template.rules: - Commit ONLY files under shared/tests/, launcher/tests/, and docs/ledger/. - Do NOT commit production source files (shared/src/, launcher/*.py non-test). - Single commit covering all new/modified test files + ledger entry.

mature_not_minimal.directive:
The 982-test floor is a minimum. The WI decomposition yields ~42 tests if all branches are covered — write all 42. Do not truncate to 20 to barely clear the floor.
Within-scope adjacent work (authorized without additional SDO approval, <= 1h): - While reading shared/schemas/car.py for WI-10, you notice a utility function not in the WIs that has zero test coverage. Add a test for it within TestDecisionArtifact or a new TestCarUtility class. - While reading shared/src/runtime_config.py, you find a 5th small function with no tests and trivial coverage cost (<= 2 tests). Add it. - Correcting a pre-existing test isolation bug discovered during test authoring (a test that leaks state into a later test) — fix the leak without changing the test assertions.
Out of scope (requires SDO sign-off): - Tests for services/policy_agent/ or services/assistant_orchestrator/ (EA-1/EA-2 territory). - Tests for services/ui_gateway/ or services/ui_shell/ (EA-3 territory, already merged). - Any production code change.
Per SDV §5.3: 962 + 20 is the floor. If substantive coverage of a domain naturally yields 50 new tests, write the 50 tests.

file.reason: Authoritative SDO continuation; EA-4 scope contract

file.sections: §5.1 items EA-4 scope, §5.2 Out-of-scope, §5.3 mature-not-minimal, §4 Success Criteria

file.reason: Predecessor ledger — format reference; baseline test count 962
REQUIRED for WI-1..WI-4 — resolve_service_root, resolve_deployment_mode, parse_deployment_mode, build_failure_fingerprint
REQUIRED for WI-10 — CAR schema, canonical_hash, is_complete, ActionVerb/Sensitivity/AdjudicationDecision enums
REQUIRED for WI-5..WI-6 — _validate_vsock_topology all failure branches; _validate_guest_runtime_configs
REQUIRED for WI-9 — request_elevation; ctypes call surface
REQUIRED for WI-11 — IPC message type convenience encoders (verify exact filename from source)
Existing test file to extend for WI-1..WI-4 (read before modifying)
Existing test file to extend for WI-5..WI-6 (read before modifying)
Existing test file to extend for WI-7..WI-8 (read before modifying; check import isolation pattern)
Existing test file to extend for WI-9 (read before modifying)
